review-details_detailsLink__NxIv5: Pros and Cons of Working in BPCL: A Comprehensive Analysis of Opportunities and Challenges
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
Working at Bharat Petroleum Corporation Limited (BPCL), like any other organization, has its own set of advantages and disadvantages. Here's a rundown of the pros and cons:

Pros:

Stability and Reputation: BPCL is a well-established company in the oil and gas sector with a strong reputation in the industry. Working for a reputable company can provide stability in terms of job security and career growth opportunities.

Competitive Salary and Benefits: BPCL generally offers competitive salaries and benefits packages to its employees, including health insurance, retirement plans, and other perks.

Training and Development: The company often invests in the training and development of its employees, providing opportunities for skill enhancement and career advancement through various programs and initiatives.

Diverse Work Environment: BPCL operates across various segments of the oil and gas industry, providing employees with exposure to diverse projects and opportunities to work in different roles and locations.

Employee Welfare Programs: BPCL typically has employee welfare programs in place to support the well-being of its workforce, including health and wellness initiatives, employee assistance programs, and work-life balance policies.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
Hierarchical Structure: Like many large organizations, BPCL may have a hierarchical structure that could potentially lead to bureaucratic processes and slow decision-making, making it challenging for some employees to navigate and implement ideas quickly.

Workload and Stress: Depending on the role and department, employees at BPCL may experience high workloads and stress, especially during peak operational periods or when working on critical projects.

Limited Flexibility: Working in the oil and gas industry, particularly in operational roles, may require adherence to strict schedules and protocols, limiting flexibility in work hours and location for some employees.

Industry Volatility: The oil and gas industry is subject to market fluctuations and geopolitical factors that can impact the company's performance and stability, potentially affecting job security and career prospects for employees.

Environmental and Ethical Concerns: As an oil and gas company, BPCL may face scrutiny and criticism regarding its environmental practices and ethical considerations related to fossil fuel consumption and climate change.

review-details_detailsLink__NxIv5: Review based on my profile and experience only. Not applicable to all BPCL employees
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
-People are technology friendly.Technology driven organization compared to normal PSUs.
-Still following the organization culture of Shell, it's parent company- professionel in most aspects.
-A solid support system involving channel partners and company employees across the country makes one feel at home, in any place within the geography of India.
-Entrusts lot of responsibilities on youngsters.
-Job involves lot of decision making and freedom
-Senior management have visionaries among them who have the ability to inspire and guide young people like us.
-Very approachable seniors. 
-Excellent training and a hand-holding during the initial days to get used to our jobs.
-Very challenging job profiles on offer.
-Of Course good money (satisfies Need, not Greed):)
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
-No HR role interactions at all especially for field staff.
-One might be posted in an up-country location wher one will be the only one posted.
-Most profiles involve a lot of travelling.
 -Strategic Planning while conduct of business seems to be lacking( my take after only over an year of experience which  may not be correct.)
-Growth not comparable to private MNCs but the experience is much more rewarding (I have experience of working in an MNC too).
-Very hectic job profiles as opposed to popular perception and result oriented appraisals keeps our anxiety levels high.
- Conservative superiors who may not be aggressive in their actions to survive in a competitive market makes us work within constraints at times.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: pay and benefits are good,
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
Although, it is a good company with Navaratna status, the managers are the ones who damage its reputation.
Senior Management:-
1) They will absolutely disregard your educational qualification and the job profile they give will be irrelevant to what you have specialized in. I know of many electrical engineers who were looking after civil jobs and mechanical engineers are expected to perform civil/electrical jobs. When asked, they'll say, all such departments are mere divisions and you have attitude problems.
2) Senior Management will aggrandize their power to subdue and undermine you. They'll ask you to stay back unnecessarily at workplace. If you have certain personal problems, it will not be considered. Juniors are dominated by seniors.
3) If you are sincere and serious about work, BPCL isnt the place for you as its all about politics, favoritism/Nepotism/Political influence taking precedence. I even know of a guy who was denied his first promotion on no solid grounds.
4) If you are a junior at workplace, seniors, instead of encouraging and training you, will threaten you and dump all their work on you. You will be rendered helpless in such situations and for any mistake they commit, you will be made the scapegoat.
5) No career prospects or future aspirations can be there in such an atmosphere.

review-details_detailsLink__NxIv5: Highly demotivating and frustrating work environment
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
Workload at most times of the year in the IT department is minimal. The 9-6 timing typical of a PSU is adhered to mostly

Availability of loans for various commodities like cars, motorcycles, furniture etc. on confirmation as an employee.

Bonds which are typical of PSU are absent.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
No incentive based pay.

When workload increases, adequate rest period and incentives are not given. It becomes like working for a Private company without any extra pay. Overtime pay, if any is a pittance.

Promotion is fixed regardless of the mettle of the employee. This claim is refuted by HR department of the company, but the fact is that seniority holds sway, and not potential.

Pay hikes are invisible. Even after working for two years, there has hardly been a increase of a few thousand bucks to the gross pay.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: Any job's basic characteristic-Job security & Pay and allowances
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
No reward/punishment.

Have to get overloaded of extra work if any non-performer is in your location or if you can work more efficiently than other in your team.

Worst remote location postings/travel even google needs to update in its map.

Intentionally preposterous transfer postings to uniquely uncomfortable locations for an individual every 3 years.

Promotions matter only in pay as one needs to do the same job for 12 years even if he is on fast track. Actual promotion - job role wise- happens in a minimum time frame of 15 years.

Anywhere near/in home state postings more than one period of posting-3 years is mostly impossible.
   
Working hours may extend more than 14 hours a day including sundays and holidays. However company policy strictly prohibit anyone to work on constitutional holidays such as Independence day and Republic Day- Kudos to work life balance.

No personality development as job is mostly monotonous and boring.

Need to consult psychiatrist frequently to avoid suicidal thoughts that arise because of great philosophical managers.

-- Unlinke all PSU, BPCL KR doesn't provide accommodation for employees. We were asked to search for acco within a week.
--  Favoritism : Hiring only from bunch of local colleges and people from these colleges are only put up in best departments with no special consideration to IITs/NITs employees.
-- Promotion: Employees from Kerala/TN are more favored for promotion and high chances to go up the ladder. And your work performance is irrelevant when it comes to promotions too.
-- Ambiguous Transfer policy : In spite of being National company, chances of transfers are minimal and most of the time it is rejected on the basis of lack of manpower.
-- Training/Mentors : There is no proper structure when it comes to training the new employees. No proper mentors are provided and you are supposed to learn everything on your own and this thing becomes harder for a person who can't speak local language. review-details_detailsLink__NxIv5: Worst place to work in India
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: Good starting Salary, status in society for having a "Goverment Job", A good place to earn corrupt money from the network of Dealers/Customers/Vendors if you are into such stuff, A good place where you can do nothing all day, but still earn same as any hard worker. Never fire anyone.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
Worst HR culture. In fact there is no culture. Only "Jugad". 

Line Managers will be people with experience at about your age with no leadership qualities. Either end micro-Managed or stranded. 

No work life balance and Managers don't respect personal Space. All senior Managers attitude is "I'm going to make sure you will suffer the same way I have all these years"

HR Dept. is just a namesake dept. where people with "jugad" end up in HQ in Mumbai and do nothing. Response from Top management after BPCL being declared least empathetic workplace in the world by Harward Business review was "there is nothing wrong with BPCL policy and HBR is wrong!"

Salary- I was recruited by BPC from Campus, with a CTC offer of 12 lac. But ended up receiving 45k per month. 

No Salary hike with Experience. Employees with 20 years experience takes away a few thousand more than a trainee.

Promotion Based on "Jugad" and not on performance. Performance Management System (PMS) is just namesake. Marks depend upon two things.  Your relation with line manager and Jugad of your colleagues. (Bell Curve structure- only one employee can get 90+mark). No review mechanism of PMS. Once you ask for review then BPCL is going to make your life miserable. Better to just go with it. 

Performance related Pay or Bonus is the funniest thing in BPCL. No employee knows what is the calculation behind the bonus he gets. A mail is generated saying "your bonus is confidential".

Transfer of Location: 1) you are not going to get where you want 2) Transfer posting is a mechanism used by BPCL as a way to hurt you if you asks them questions.
